nodes.label: Official Trading Card Grading Service (PSA Services Page)
PSA 10 – Gem Mint
nodes.rationale: Virtually perfect card: four perfectly sharp corners, sharp focus, full original gloss, no staining, image centered 55/45 or better on front and 75/25 on reverse. Minor printing imperfection allowed if it does not impair overall appeal.

nodes.label: PSA 9 – Mint
nodes.rationale: Superb condition; only one allowed minor flaw: very slight wax stain on reverse, minor printing imperfection, or slightly off-white borders. Centering 60/40 or better front, 90/10 back.

nodes.label: PSA 8 – NM-MT
nodes.rationale: Near Mint-Mint. Appears Mint at first glance; may have very slight wax stain on reverse, slightest fraying at one or two corners, minor printing imperfection, slightly off-white borders. Centering 65/35 front, 90/10 back.

nodes.label: PSA 7 – NM
nodes.rationale: Near Mint. Slight surface wear visible upon close inspection; slight fraying on some corners; picture focus may be slightly off-register; minor printing blemish acceptable; slight wax staining on back only; most original gloss retained. Centering 70/30 front, 90/10 back.

nodes.label: PSA 6 – EX-MT
nodes.rationale: Excellent-Mint. Visible surface wear or printing defect not detracting from appeal; very light scratch only under close inspection; slightly graduated fraying at corners; minor wax stain on reverse; very slight notching on edges; some off-whiteness on borders. Centering 80/20 front, 90/10 back.

nodes.label: PSA 5 – EX
nodes.rationale: Excellent. Very minor rounding of corners; surface wear or printing defects more visible; minor chipping on edges; more apparent loss of gloss; focus slightly off-register; several light scratches visible on close inspection. Centering 85/15 front, 90/10 back.

nodes.label: PSA 4 – VG-EX
nodes.rationale: Very Good-Excellent. Corners slightly rounded; noticeable but modest surface wear; light scuffing or scratches; some original gloss retained; borders slightly off-white; light crease may be visible. Centering 85/15 front, 90/10 back.

nodes.label: PSA 3 – VG
nodes.rationale: Very Good. Some rounding of corners; apparent surface wear; possible light scuffing or scratches; focus may be somewhat off-register; edges exhibit noticeable wear; much original gloss lost; borders may be yellowed; crease may be visible; slight stain on obverse. Centering 90/10 front and back.

nodes.label: PSA 2 – GOOD
nodes.rationale: Good. Corners show accelerated rounding; obvious surface wear; scratching, scuffing, light staining, or chipping; several creases; no original gloss; considerable discoloration. Centering 90/10 front and back.

nodes.label: PSA 1.5 – FR
nodes.rationale: Fair. Extreme corner wear possibly affecting framing; advanced surface wear including scuffing, scratching, pitting, chipping, and staining; picture possibly quite out-of-register; borders brown and dirty; one or more heavy creases; card must be fully intact. Centering 90/10 front and back.

nodes.label: PSA 1 – PR
nodes.rationale: Poor. Same qualities as PSA 1.5 but defects advanced to the point where eye appeal has nearly vanished. May be missing one or two small pieces, exhibit major creasing nearly breaking through all cardboard layers, contain extreme discoloration/dirtiness, or show noticeable warping.

nodes.label: PSA Grade Qualifiers (MK, OC, ST, PD, OF)
nodes.rationale: Qualifiers applied to PSA grades to note specific defects: MK=Marks (writing, ink, pencil marks), OC=Off-Center, ST=Staining, PD=Print Defect, OF=Out of Focus.

nodes.label: PSA No-Grade Codes (N1-N9)
nodes.rationale: Cards returned without a numeric grade: N1=Trimming, N2=Restoration, N3=Recoloration, N4=Questionable Authenticity, N5=Altered Stock, N6=Minimum Size, N7=Cleaning, N8=Miscut, N9=Don't Grade. Some qualify for Authentic Altered encapsulation.

nodes.label: PSA Eye Appeal and Subjectivity in Grading
nodes.rationale: PSA grading is mostly objective (centering, defects, staining) but has a subjective component tied to market acceptability and eye appeal. Graders may deviate from strict centering guidelines when overall eye appeal strongly favors a higher or lower grade than the measured centering would indicate.

nodes.label: PSA Authentication and Encapsulation Process
nodes.rationale: Three-step process: (1) Authentication – verify genuineness and check for doctoring such as re-coloring or trimming; (2) Grading – assess condition on 1-10 scale if authentic; (3) Encapsulation – seal in sonically sealed tamper-evident case with PSA LightHouse label showing grade and certification number.

nodes.label: PSA Grading Service Tiers and Pricing
PSA Population Report
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use PSA and BGS Pop Reports to Your Advantage.md
nodes.author: SundoCards
nodes.rationale: A publicly accessible database tracking every card PSA has graded, broken down by set, player, and grade. Updated continuously as new cards are submitted. Primary use is assessing rarity and informing buy/sell/hold decisions. Data only reflects graded cards — not raw cards, not cards graded by other companies.

nodes.label: BGS (Beckett Grading Services)
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use PSA and BGS Pop Reports to Your Advantage.md
nodes.author: SundoCards

nodes.label: BGS Population Report
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use PSA and BGS Pop Reports to Your Advantage.md
nodes.author: SundoCards
nodes.rationale: Beckett's equivalent to the PSA Population Report, tracking every card BGS has graded. Same limitations apply: only reflects submitted cards, can be inflated by cross-grading and resubmission.

nodes.label: Population Report Rarity Assessment
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use PSA and BGS Pop Reports to Your Advantage.md
nodes.author: SundoCards
nodes.rationale: Low population in a high grade signals rarity and potential value. Must be weighed against demand — a rare card of an unpopular player has limited value. Grade distribution (e.g., how many PSA 10s vs PSA 9s) is more informative than total population alone.

nodes.label: Crack and Resubmit (Pop Report Inflation)
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use the PSA Population Report to Invest.md
nodes.rationale: Practice of removing a card from its graded slab and resubmitting to PSA hoping for a higher grade. Can artificially inflate population counts if the same physical card gets counted under multiple certification numbers. A known limitation of pop report accuracy.

nodes.label: Grade Distribution Analysis (Pop Reports)
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use the PSA Population Report to Invest.md
nodes.rationale: Analyzing the breakdown of grades (e.g., how many PSA 10s vs 9s vs 8s) is more valuable than looking at total population. A card with 20,000 total graded copies but only 50 PSA 10s is still scarce at the top grade. Vintage cards often show this pattern due to decades of handling.

nodes.label: Supply-Demand Intersection for Card Valuation
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use the PSA Population Report to Invest.md
nodes.rationale: Card value is determined at the intersection of low supply (low pop count at a given grade) and high demand (player popularity, iconic status). A low pop count alone does not create value — demand is required. Blue-chip cards like Jordan or Charizard can retain high value even with large populations because demand is nearly limitless.

nodes.label: Junk Wax Era (Late 1980s-Early 1990s)
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use the PSA Population Report to Invest.md
nodes.rationale: Cards from the late 1980s and early 1990s were printed in massive quantities, leading to high population counts and generally lower per-card value. Submission rates from this era further inflate pop report numbers.

nodes.label: PSA Certification Number (Slab ID)
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use the PSA Population Report to Invest.md
nodes.rationale: Unique identifier printed on each PSA slab. Used to look up an individual card's details in the PSA Population Report database. eBay and other marketplaces integrate cert number lookup to automatically display grade and population info in listings.

nodes.label: Market Report: Population Perspective (PSA Article by Joe Orlando)
nodes.author: Joe Orlando

nodes.label: Population Report Contextual Limitations
nodes.author: Joe Orlando
nodes.rationale: Pop numbers must be analyzed in context: (1) Low pop may reflect infrequent submission, not genuine rarity. (2) Pre-war cards with low pop are legitimately scarce; modern low-pop cards may simply be unsubmitted. (3) Numbers exclude rejected cards (trimmed, altered, questionable). (4) Most raw cards are lower grade — high-grade bias means the actual distribution skews far lower than pop reports suggest. Treating pop report as gospel leads to costly mistakes.

nodes.label: SGC Grading Scale
nodes.rationale: SGC uses a numeric scale from 1 to 10 with half-point increments plus a 10 PRI (Pristine) tier above 10 GM. 20 distinct grade levels. Uses quality labels like Pristine, Gem Mint, Mint+, Mint, NM/MT+, NM/MT, NM+, NM, EX/NM+, EX/NM, EX+, EX, VG/EX+, VG/EX, VG+, VG, Good+, Good, Fair, Poor.

nodes.label: SGC 10 PRI – Pristine
nodes.rationale: Virtually flawless card. Requires 50/50 centering, crisp focus, four sharp corners, free of stains, no breaks in surface gloss, no print or refractor lines, and no visible wear under magnification. Stricter than PSA 10 which allows 55/45 centering and minor printing imperfections.

nodes.label: SGC 6 – EX/NM
nodes.rationale: Excellent/Near Mint. 75/25 or better centering, slight fuzzing of corners may be evident, skewed cut may be more evident, focus or register may be off, and slight notching of edges may exist.

nodes.label: SGC 5.5 – EX+
nodes.rationale: An Excellent card that exhibits high-end overall quality and eye appeal. Intermediate grade between EX and EX/NM.

nodes.label: Print Lines (Card Defect)
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use PSA and BGS Pop Reports to Your Advantage.md
nodes.author: SundoCards
nodes.rationale: Fine linear marks on card surface caused during manufacturing (roller pressure inconsistencies, ink distribution variations, print drum issues). Not caused by post-production handling. Can affect grading: minor lines may not affect grade significantly; prominent lines lower the grade. Cannot be removed without risking further damage.

nodes.label: Cross-Grading (BGS to PSA or vice versa)
nodes.source_file: docs/cards/raw/How to Use PSA and BGS Pop Reports to Your Advantage.md
nodes.author: SundoCards
nodes.rationale: Submitting a card graded by one company to a different grading company. Inflates population counts across both companies for a single physical card. E.g., a PSA 9 submitted to BGS hoping for a BGS 9.5 now appears in both companies' population reports.
